title: Cassini's Close Encounter with Phoebe
story: Zooming through space, the Cassini-Huygens spacecraft made history in 2004. On June 11, it swooped incredibly close to Phoebe, a mysterious moon of Saturn. This flyby gave scientists their best-ever look at the small, icy world. Phoebe's surface appeared dark and cratered, hinting at its ancient past. The mission's detailed images and data helped unlock secrets of the outer solar system.

title: A Ship Takes Shape in the Wilderness
story: Hammers rang out in the remote wilderness of Nootka Sound, British Columbia. On June 11, 1788, workers began building the first British ship on the Pacific coast. This small vessel would open up a new world of trade and exploration for the British in the vast waters of the Pacific Ocean.

title: Cricket's Lowest Score Ever
story: Bowler George Dennett had a day for the record books in 1907. On June 11, he and teammate Gilbert Jessop teamed up to dismiss the Northamptonshire cricket team for a mere 12 runs. This remains the lowest total ever scored in a first-class cricket match. Dennett's incredible bowling performance left the opposing team stunned and secured his place in cricket history.
